essence: David directs the agent to build a presentable single-page slide deck covering Epic 4 plus its retrospective and KDD changes, with standard index pages as tabs in both JSON documents, after loading and understanding the style files.

actions.text: Build Epic 4 + retrospective deck with KDD changes, standard index tabs in both JSON documents, positioned after Epic 4
Load and fully understand style files (components, main colors) before deck work

essence: David decides to consolidate all Epic 4 information (original plus retrospective conversations) into one detailed semantic JSON document via a background process before generating any new slide decks, index page, or tabs.

actions.text: Run background process to consolidate original + retrospective Epic 4 conversations into a single semantic JSON document before slide creation

essence: Amid movie background, David issues agent tasks for the ITO voice-input research: write the technology validation report, clarify whether PV recorder's commercial pre-compiled license requires payment, update the documentation, and report (not fix) where the research docs are muddied so alignment improves before building.

actions.text: Write the technology validation report for the voice-input tool research
Clarify PV recorder 'commercial pre-compiled' licensing (payment required?)
Report muddied/inconsistent aspects across the research docs without fixing them

essence: David questions whether the ITO project's tech choices are validated before building further — specifically whether Rust was necessary at all or everything could have been done in JavaScript, and whether better technology options exist.

actions.text: Validate ITO tech stack: was Rust needed vs JavaScript, and are the chosen technologies the best options

essence: David scopes a two-app voice-input system modeled on the open-source ITO project — a Rust tool mapping the Mac Fn key to microphone capture (considering Mac audio/MIDI virtual devices) and a JavaScript app sending speech to his Groq Whisper inference endpoint — insisting on requirements documents first, and flags that the agent wrongly drafted a build plan and wrote it to the wrong folder despite his plan-location settings.

actions.text: Research the ITO open-source project's architecture (Fn key access, microphone/MIDI hookup) as the basis for requirements
Write two linked requirements documents (Rust Fn-key/mic tool + JavaScript Groq-processing app) instead of building now
Investigate why plan files ignore the global plan-location setting and land in the wrong folder

essence: Chiang Mai core group works hands-on processing three OMI meeting transcripts through an 8-agent chunk/synthesis pipeline extracting questions and open loops into structured CSV/JSONL for the LLM council, then locks in a Jan 24 test meetup at the new venue (300-600 baht/hr, options up to 50-60 people), discusses possibly renting a larger AI coworking hub, and agrees to an online call with Marcus about folding his meetup group into a transparent steering-committee model.

actions.text: Run the Saturday Jan 24 test meetup at the new venue (locked in)
Schedule online call with Marcus next week re group ownership, governance, and folding his Saturday meetup into the shared model
Extract questions + open loops (plus any third category) from the three transcripts into CSV/JSONL machine-readable output

essence: The Chiang Mai four-person core agrees to govern their open-source AI community via an LLM council (Karpathy's LLM Council repo) acting as board of directors with humans as steering committee — disagreements become anonymous 'point to the council' items, transcripts feed in via OMI, principles (like accessibility) over hard rules, group name (Compact/Hive/Lurk/North Star) delegated to the council — while side threads cover Ultra Think's removal from Claude, VibeDeck/FliDeck stream-deck hardware with custom skins, and transparent donation-based funding for council inference costs.

actions.text: Adopt Karpathy's LLM Council repo as the base and start feeding meeting transcripts into it
Let the council pick the group name from the shortlist (Compact, Hive, Loom, Lurk, North Star)
Build a transparent UI/dashboard surfacing council decisions, tracked questions, and anonymous disagreements

essence: After a session David offers a fellow developer one-on-one mentoring at his place anytime to work through their questions and problems.

Post-session group talk where David teaches the create-review-document agent loop (BMAD reviewers, KDD as the project's learning second-brain, design systems needing both positive and negative specs), then coaches a friend burned by a ChatGPT-designed closed-loop outreach funnel to drop algorithm gaming and post consistent daily 20-minute value videos on YouTube as a slow asset, offering to help systematize his outreach.

actions.text: Help the friend systematize his YouTube posting and simple email outreach (offer made)

essence: David hosts week 1 of a 50-week vibe coding series in Chiang Mai, live-scaffolding a reusable TypeScript/React/Vite/Tailwind/Express/Socket.IO web-app harness with Claude Code (dashboard + command palette, config JSON not DB, swagger-style API docs, calculator as first hosted tool) while teaching context engineering, lean CLAUDE.md plus on-demand skills, Rust-vs-JS lessons from the VibeDeck attempt, and BMAD retrospective agents.

actions.text: Continue the 50-week harness series: add one small app per weekly session on top of the week-1 harness

essence: David preps the next two-hour Languish the Artist session with Voz — reviewing the v-Voz repo docs and last Loom summary, demanding an analysis (not fixes) of the story 4.3 mock generator and the unreadable 4.1-4.3 command cheat sheets, and drafting a scheduling email offering Wednesday or Thursday with both Voz's and Thailand time zones — while flagging the assistant used the wrong client directory/email system.

actions.text: Analyze the story 4.3 mock generator (images 3-5): what it does, why it falls short, proposed better approach — before changing anything
Draft scheduling email to Voz offering Wednesday or Thursday with both time zones shown
Fix readability of story 4.1-4.3 command cheat sheets and update the audit trail from the correct Voz client directory

loop.label: Voz Languish the Artist next session
